testPageVerification.details: Test page contains 6 takt/tabs block instances with 21 total tabs-item children across all variations. Raw block markup confirmed via taktician_wp_raw_content.

build.details: Build compiles successfully (webpack compiled with 3 warnings: bundle size limits only, no errors)

lint.details: 4 lint issues in Tabs block files (auto-fixable prettier formatting). 1 react-hooks/exhaustive-deps warning in Tabs.tsx (useEffect missing deps). All are auto-fixable or non-blocking.

issues.message: useEffect has missing dependencies: attributes.tabsDescription, attributes.tabsHeading, innerBlocksLength, setAttributes

registration.details: Both block.json files valid. Parent: takt/tabs (apiVersion 3, category takt-theme, supports anchor). Child: takt/tabs-item (apiVersion 3, parent: [takt/tabs], supports anchor). All required files exist: block.json, index.tsx, component TSX, PHP template, SVG icon for both parent and child. Theme auto-registers via inc/helpers/blocks.php.

attributes.details: Parent attributes: anchor (string), eyebrow (string), heading (string), description (string), buttons (array), tabsHeading (array, default []), tabsDescription (array, default []). Child attributes: anchor (string), tabTitle (string), tabDescription (string). All match spec. tabsHeading/tabsDescription auto-synced from child attributes via useEffect. Test page raw content confirms attributes save correctly with proper values.

accessibility.details: Desktop: tablist with role=tablist and aria-orientation=vertical rendered in PHP. Tab buttons have role=tab, aria-selected, tabindex, and unique IDs. Frontend JS (Tabs.js) sets up ARIA dynamically: aria-controls on tab buttons, role=tabpanel + tabindex=0 + aria-labelledby on content panels. Keyboard navigation: ArrowUp/Down, Home/End, Enter/Space all handled. Mobile: JS removes tabpanel roles on mobile mode, using native details/summary semantics instead. Summary elements have proper list-none and marker hiding CSS. Responsive mode switching correctly transitions ARIA between desktop tablist and mobile accordion patterns.

edgeCases.details: Test page covers: full content (4 tabs, buttons, description), blue accent (3 tabs, no buttons), minimal (heading only, 2 tabs), many tabs (8 tabs edge case), long content (multiple sub-headings), green accent (2 tabs with descriptions). Mobile descriptions appear only when tabDescription is non-empty (confirmed via page audit: 4 out of 21 items have .tabs-item-description). Empty optional fields handled correctly.

markupDuplication.details: No content duplication. The block uses a single set of <details> elements for both desktop and mobile. Desktop tablist buttons are a separate control mechanism (they contain tab titles from the parent's tabsHeading array, which are synced copies of child tabTitle attributes - this is a navigation control, not content duplication). The desktop title inside tabs-item-content is hidden on mobile via 'hidden md:block', and the summary is hidden on desktop via 'md:!hidden'. This is the details/summary pattern where each viewport shows the appropriate control surface. No JS-based cloneNode or innerHTML duplication.

tsxPhpSync.details: TSX and PHP structures match with 2 known intentional differences documented below.

intentionalDifferences.description: md:h-full class on tabs-panels container is editor-only (TSX has it, PHP does not)
intentionalDifferences.reason: Editor needs full-height panels for proper visual layout in the block editor. Frontend JS manages panel visibility via details open attribute instead.

intentionalDifferences.description: Mobile description paragraph is frontend-only (PHP has it, TSX does not)
intentionalDifferences.reason:
The mobile description (

) renders the tabDescription inside the accordion content area on mobile. In the editor, descriptions are edited inline in the parent's tab button area via AdvancedRichText.

syncedElements: Tab buttons: identical class structure with conditional bg-accent/selected
Details wrapper: identical class_name arrays on tabs-item
Summary: identical classes with md:!hidden and marker hiding

innerBlocks.details: Parent allows only takt/tabs-item. Template: [['takt/tabs-item']]. Custom Appender for adding new tabs. Child allows any blocks (no restriction). Child template: [['core/paragraph']]. Content renders correctly in both summary (mobile) and content panel (desktop). Test page confirms various inner block types render: paragraphs, headings (h4), lists.

specCompliance.details: All 6 test page variations match the spec. Variation 1 (Yellow/Full Content): 4 tabs with buttons and descriptions. Variation 2 (Blue): 3 tabs, no buttons. Variation 3 (Minimal/Neon-Green): heading only, 2 tabs. Variation 4 (Orange/Many Tabs): 8 tabs edge case. Variation 5 (Purple/Long Content): long content with sub-headings. Variation 6 (Green): 2 tabs with descriptions. Tab counts match spec. Accent colors applied via wrapper group classes. tabsHeading and tabsDescription arrays correctly synced.

issues.description: Prettier formatting errors in Tabs.tsx and TabsItem.tsx (4 errors total). Auto-fixable with bun run lint:fix.

React Hook useEffect in Tabs.tsx (line 82) has missing dependencies: attributes.tabsDescription, attributes.tabsHeading, innerBlocksLength, setAttributes. This is a common pattern in WordPress blocks where adding all deps would cause infinite re-renders, but should be acknowledged.

fix.suggested: Consider adding an eslint-disable-next-line comment to suppress the warning if this is intentional behavior.

passedChecks: Build compiles without errors
Test page contains 6 block variations with 21 total tab items
Both parent (takt/tabs) and child (takt/tabs-item) blocks register correctly
All attributes defined with correct types and defaults
tabsHeading and tabsDescription arrays auto-sync from child attributes
Desktop tablist has role=tablist with aria-orientation=vertical
Tab buttons have role=tab, aria-selected, tabindex, and unique IDs
Frontend JS sets up aria-controls and role=tabpanel dynamically
Keyboard navigation: ArrowUp/Down, Home/End, Enter/Space all implemented
Mobile mode removes tabpanel roles, uses native details/summary semantics
Responsive mode switching handles ARIA transitions correctly
No content duplication - single <details> elements serve both viewports
TSX and PHP structures match (2 intentional differences documented)
md:h-full editor-only class confirmed as intentional
Mobile description paragraph frontend-only confirmed as intentional
Inner blocks render correctly (paragraphs, headings, lists)
All 6 accent color variations work (yellow, blue, neon-green, orange, purple, green)
Empty state handling works (minimal variation has only heading)
Many tabs edge case (8 tabs) renders correctly
Long content in tab renders correctly with sub-headings
SVG icons present for both parent and child blocks (24x24, integer coordinates)
